title: Ingrown toenail
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: An ingrown toenail is a common problem where the nail grows into the toe and may cause pain, redness, and swelling.
Home care may include warm salty-water soaks, keeping the foot dry, wide comfortable shoes, and label-safe pain medicine.
Patients should not cut the toenail, pick at the toe, or wear tight pointy shoes while trying home care.
Patients should seek medical care for pus, feverish symptoms, failed home care, diabetes, or need for antibiotics or podiatry treatment.
